expectations.text: El summary incluye los dos action items concretos: que Alex lidere el architecture sync y que Sarah confirme presupuesto para la conferencia

expectations.text: El coaching.md contiene preguntas de tipo socrático (no afirmaciones, sino preguntas abiertas)
expectations.evidence: The coaching file contains 8 open-ended questions, all ending with '?'. Examples: 'how much of the direction came from you versus from Alex, and what does that tell you about whose 1on1 it was?'; 'What would have happened if you had first asked what makes it hard for them to raise concerns earlier — what might you have learned that you didn't?'; 'What does that tell you about how Alex understands the scope of their current role, and what responsibility do you have in either expanding or clarifying that?'

expectations.text: El summary NO contiene juicios privados sobre el manager ni áreas de mejora del manager
expectations.evidence: The summary (shareable/2026-03-15-alex-summary.md) contains no judgments about Sarah as a manager and no improvement areas. It only contains factual meeting content: topics discussed, quotes, agreements, and action items. Private analysis (Manager Improvement Areas, Radical Candor Analysis, etc.) is exclusively in the coaching/analysis file, not in the shareable summary.

suggestions: The coaching.md 'Final Reflection' section consists of a paragraph of statements rather than questions — consider whether it should also follow the Socratic format or be explicitly labeled as a synthesis/reflection section to avoid ambiguity.

Assertion 9 could be made stricter: it asks for 'the two concrete action items' but the summary actually contains three action items (Alex flagging concerns + Alex leading sync + Sarah confirming budget). The assertion only checks for two of them. Future versions could verify the complete action item set.

The summary correctly omits manager improvement areas, but the analysis file contains rich private feedback. A future assertion could verify the analysis file is explicitly marked as 'Manager's use only' (it is — both coaching and analysis files carry the warning).
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect pass rate — all 11 assertions pass. The skill produced three well-structured files with correct naming, language (English), type labeling (career), Otter.ai-formatted timestamps, a Career & Development section, checkout flow feedback reference, both required action items, Socratic coaching questions, and clean separation between shareable and private content.
